texts.full_text: Seidman, PhD. Located within the Departments of Genetics at Harvard Medical School and the Cardiovascular Division of Brigham and Women’s Hospital and supported by Howard Hughes Medical Institute and the National Institutes of Health, the laboratory harnesses and integrates clinical medicine and molecular technologies to define disease-causing gene mutations and genetic variations that increase disease risk. The laboratory also develops and interrogates genetically engineered mouse models and CRISPR/Cas9 genome-edited human induced pluripotent stem cells to elucidate pathogenetic mechanisms for disease. Majorareas of focus in the lab include genetic mechanisms underlying cardiovascular diseases, with particular attention to cardiac arrhythmias and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. By combining cutting-edge genetic research with in-depth clinical study, the Seidman Lab seeks to elucidate the molecular mechanisms of disease, offering potential pathways to diagnostic and therapeutic advances. The lab engages in several core research themes, including: 1. The molecular genetics of cardiac and skeletal muscle disorders – studying genetic causes of disorders, such as muscular dystrophies and cardiomyopathies, and understanding the molecular pathways implicated in disease pathogenesis. 2. Genomic investigation of cardiac and skeletal muscle – utilizing genomic and proteomic approaches to discover new genetic associations and pathways that influence disease. 3. Genome editing technologies – utilizing the CRISPR/Cas9 system to investigate the functions of gene mutations and develop gene therapy approaches. Through this integrative and multidisciplinary approach, the Seidman Lab not only seeks to develop novel models of disease but also to bridge the gap between basic genetic understanding and clinical application, ultimately improving patient care and genetic counseling. For the broader community, the lab provides resources and maintains several collaborative and outreach initiatives to disseminate genetic discoveries and promote further research. These initiatives, including the Next Generation Data and collaborative tools, support the work of many scientists and researchers across the globe. For the latest updates and access to publications, lab resources, and ongoing research initiatives, please explore further sections of the Seidman Lab's website. Cornelius Hi, my name is cornelius Beaver but i like to be called by my nickname "Ned". My background includes a degree in Environmental Science from the University of Guelph (Ontario) where we learned about sustainable agriculture practices and how they can help us live more sustainably. After graduation, Ned worked as an environmental consultant for several years before joining S.H.O.W. I have always been passionate about food systems change - particularly those that are equitable to all people in society regardless of their socio-economic status or background. My role at the Lillie St Urban Garden is a perfect fit. I am responsible mainly with coordinating our educational programs and workshops, which include everything from gardening basics for beginners through advanced topics like permaculture design. I believe that education can be one powerful tool in changing people's perceptions about food systems - especially when it comes to urban agriculture. My goal at S.H.O.W is not only teach the community how we grow our own vegetables and fruits, but also empower them with knowledge on sustainable practices so they too could start their gardens. I am excited for what lies ahead as I work alongside my fellow staff members. We are all passionate about food systems change - particularly those that promote equity in access to healthy foods. texts.full_text: rriage of Hindu widows in all jurisdictions of India under East India Company rule. It was drafted by Lord Dalhousie and passed by Lord Dalhousie before the Indian Rebellion of 1857. It was the first major social reform legislation after the abolition of Sati by Lord William Bentinck. To protect what it considered family honour and family property, upper-caste Hindu society had long disallowed the remarriage of widows, even child and adolescent ones, all of whom were expected to live a life of austerity and abnegation. The Hindu Widows' Remarriage Act of 1856, provided legal safeguards against loss of certain forms of inheritance for remarrying a Hindu widow, though, under the Act, the widow forsook any inheritance rights she may have had in her deceased husband's property and family assets. The passage of this act was significant as it marked one major step towards women empowerment by giving them back their basic right to remarriage after being widowed at a young age often due circumstances beyond control, thus bringing an end the cruel tradition

texts.full_text: ut of order. (Easy to show because negating two numbers exchanges their ordering.) This mapping explicitly shows the symmetry (duality) between less-than and greater-than in this context. So, for any A, the number of inversions equals the number of non-inversions in ~A. But for every possible A, there corresponds exactly one ~A; when the numbers are chosen uniformly, both A and ~A are equally likely. Therefore the expected number of inversions in A equals the expected number of inversions in ~A, because these expectations are being calculated over the exact same space. Therefore the expected number of inversions in A equals the expected number of non-inversions. The sum of these expectations is the expectation of the sum, which is the constant n*(n-1)/2, or the total number of pairs. [Update 2] A simpler symmetry: For any array A of n elements, define ~A as the same elements but in reverse order. Associate the element at position i in A with the element at position n+1-i in ~A. (That is, associate each element with itself in the reversed array.) Now any inversion in A is associated with a non-inversion in ~A, just as with the construction in Update 1 above. So the same argument applies: The number of inversions in A equals the number of inversions in ~A; both A and ~A are equally likely sequences; etc. The point of the intuition here is that the "less than" and "greater than" operators are just mirror images of each other, which you can see either by negating the arguments (as in Update 1) or by swapping them (as in Update 2). In this regard, the NCQA sets standards for the quality of health care and service that health care plans provide to their members and, to the extent a health plan meets certain standards, such plan receives accreditation by NCQA. texts.full_text: sis. Nutritional findings were compared with those obtained in healthy age- and sex-matched controls, and were also related to clinical and radiographic features. All patients received chemotherapy under supervision in hospital for 8 weeks, and nutritional status was monitored at 4-week intervals. Weight, muscle mass and function, subcutand serum albumin levels of the Malawian pulmonary tuberculosis (PTB)患者 were compared before treatment initiation and after each four week interval during their eight-week chemotherapy regimen. The study aimed to assess whether there is a correlation between initial nutritional status markers such as weight loss or reduced muscle strength, which could potentially impact response rates to standard PTB therapy. Nutritional deficiencies can compromise immune function making it more difficult for patients with TB to fight off the infection and may also slow down recovery from side effects of chemotherapy. Anthropometric measurements included height (when available), mid-upper arm circumference as an indicator of muscle mass, weight loss assessment over time among others while hand-grip dynamometry provided a measure of muscular strength. Serum albumin levels were analyzed because it reflects protein status which is indicative of overall nutrition and hydration state in the body. Results from these measurements indicated significant differences between patients with PTB compared to healthy controls regarding their nutritional statuses, suggesting that malnutrition was prevalent among this cohort prior starting treatment for tuberculosis. Moreover, correlations could be observed where pre-treatment muscle mass or hand-grip strength seemed associated poorer response outcomes based on radiographic improvement and clinical symptoms resolution rates after completing chemotherapy. However studies would need to explore causality between these variables. During the follow-up assessments conducted every four weeks throughout their treatment course allowed monitoring changes in nutritional markers which could give insight into whether patients were recovering adequately or if additional interventions aimed at improving nutrition might be necessary alongside conventional anti-TB drugs regimen. The findings from this study emphasize importance of incorporating assessment

texts.full_text: National Garden Scheme (NGS) open weekend, there’s an ongoing dance between nature's untamed clockwork rhythms—the burst growth from generous sunlight and moisture—and my own human planning which at times feels sluggish by comparison. Much like a filmmaker awaiting their big premiere amidst hours spent in intimate dialogue with footage backdrops fading seasons; every gardener finds themselves looking ahead toward one spotlighted weekend of judgment against months if not years underfoot amongst nippy April chills to sizzling summer days. Onward, I'm channeling an excitement akin yet perhaps less measured than the meticulous tending from planting buds into full-flowered stars set aglow. The garden blooms defiantly towards its climactic scenes within prescribed calendar pages while simultaneously defying prediction for natural drama it bestows both onlookers and owner alike amidst these final stages leading up to showtime. texts.full_text: fluids in clinical use may have an impact on electrolyte concentration and metabolic balance and should be considered as powerful pharmacological agents. There is a growing body of evidence that fluid therapy should be more individualised and preferably based on balanced solutions. We sought to investigate the impact of three commonly used balanced fluids on electrolytes and metabolic equilibrium in an in vitro setting. Study group comprised 32 healthy male volunteers (without history of any acute/chronic disorder or known metabolic abnormality), aged 21-35 (29 ± 4) years, weight 59-103 (81.2 ± 9.8) kg, from whom blood samples were withdrawn. The whole blood was diluted in 4:1 ratio with the study solutions to make an end-concentration of 20 vol.% of each solution. The test solutions included balanced crystalloid (Plasmalyte®, Baxter, Poland [PL]), succinylated gelatin (Geloplasma®, Fresenius Kabi, Poland [GEL]) and 6% HES 130/0.4 (Volulyte®, Fresenius Kabi, Poland [HES]). All fluids caused comparable degree of haemodilution. The International Association of Directional Drilling (IADD) is a 501(c)(6) not-for-profit organization dedicated to promoting directional drilling, as well as advancing the technology, competence and knowledge of its members. IADD is also focused on helping the idrilling industry develop and promote safety standards. The association was established in 1987 and has since grown into a global network of professionals dedicated to the directional drilling field. IADD's mission is to provide a forum for its members to exchange ideas, collaborate on projects, and share best practices in the directional drilling industry. texts.full_text: T E R S--T O--T H E--E D IT O R++ Monday, March 9, 2015 LEICA SHOWS NEW OPTICAL PRODUCTS At the recentIWA 2015 in Nuremberg,Leica presented presenting a whole range of new products for hunters and nature enthusiasts. The highlight of the show were a new member of the Magnus family, the 1.8–12× 50 power zoomriflescope for universal hunting needs. In addition to the new Geovid HD-R (Types 402 and 403) and the three new UltravidHD-Plus 50 models, visitors to the Leica stand atIWA could also take alook at the latest additions to the Sport Optics line. The new Leica Noctivid binoculars, known for their exceptional low-light performance, were among these, although they weren't officially launched untillater that year. The 1.8–12 × 50 Magnus riflescope attracted a lot of attention due to its versatile magnificationrange and excellent opticalquality, making it suitable for various hunting scenarios from dawn till dusk. The Geovid HD-R rangefinding binoculars, now in their updated versions (Types 402 and 403), offer improved accuracy and reliability in ranging, crucial for precision shooting. The Ultravid HD-Plus 50 modelswere also a hit with nature enthusiasts, offering high-definition opticsand robust construction, ideal forobserving wildlife at a distance. texts.full_text: try to be as organized as possible, you will sometimes end up having a hard time trying to find a file you need. And because you shouldn’t waste time on such trivial tasks, you need to have a powerful desktop search tool by your side – like Lookeen. This user friendly, fast, and powerful search tool will find whatever file you’re looking for. It doesn’t matter where the file is stored. Lookeen will quickly find it and present it to you. The system requirements for running Lookeen are quite low: 700MHz processor or higher, at least 128MB of RAM (for Windows XP/Vista/7), and 250 MB of free hard disk space. Additionally, the software is compatible with most popular operating systems including Windows XP, Vista, 7, 8, and macOS. Lookeen operates by indexing all files on your computer's file system which allows for fast searching even within large numbers of documents or media files. The index does not take up much space compared to the actual size of data being indexed; it is like having a well-organized library card catalog that points directly to where each book (or in this case, document) is located. What sets Lookeen apart from other search tools on your computer or online services is its speed and accuracy. With its advanced indexing system, the program can quickly narrow down potential matches based upon a wide array of factors including file type, size, creation date, modification date, author name, content (keywords), comments, tags/labels, or even certain phrases contained within the documents themselves. The user interface is designed to be intuitive and easy-to-use. Once Lookeen has indexed your files, you can perform a search by entering specific words related to what you're looking for into its minimalistic yet powerful search bar. Results are presented almost instantly in an order that reflects their relevance to the query entered. In addition to its primary function

texts.full_text: 1 msec ;; SERVER: 10.10.2.10#53(10.10.2.10) ;; WHEN: Wed May 7 15:08:53 2014 ;; MSG SIZE rcvd: 464 A: When you query a caching DNS server (ra flag set in your response), what you get in the ADDITIONAL section should be considered "best effort" outside of what the RFCs explicitly require a server to do. Some servers disable the ADDITIONAL section entirely except where required by RFC, i.e. BIND's minimal-responses option. In this scenario, the ADDITIONAL section will contain records that the server is passively aware of, but you cannot consider this information to be exhaustive. It's not going to go out of its way to get the extra data for you because it would be unnecessary work and it has better things to do with its time. AAAA nameserver IPs are not frequently requested and it is common for them to be absent. Take the following example: $ rpm -q bind97 bind97-9.7.0-17.P2.el5_9.1 $ dig @localhost +noall +additional serverfault.com brad.ns.cloudflare.com. 82084 IN A 173.245.59.105 roxy.ns.cloudflare.com. 6209 IN A 173.245.58.142 $ dig @localhost +short brad.ns.cloudflare.com AAAA 2400:cb00:2049:1::adf5:3b69 $ dig @localhost +noall +additional serverfault.com brad.ns.cloudflare.com. 82056 IN A 173.245.59.105 brad.ns.cloudflare.com. 86397 IN AAAA 2400:cb00:2049:1::adf5:3b69 roxy.ns.cloudflare.com. 6181 IN A 173.245.58.142 What I've done here is demonstrate my cache returning an ADDITIONAL section that lacks the AAAA records. texts.full_text: lood damaging effects of artificial perfusion devices such as assist devices, heart valve prostheses, for example, must be evaluated in vitro before being used in the clinical setting. For this purpose, mainly animal blood has been used, and a number of associated problems are currently being discussed. Differences in the use of the term hemolysis--meaning breakdown of erythrocytes or increased plasma hemoglobin, result in incompatibility among different authors. In addition, subhemolytic damage and its quantification has not been investigated to any extent. Another problem are the differences in the mechanical fragility of erythrocytes from different animal species, and the question of transferability to the in vivo situation. Furthermore, the variability of mechanical stability within a given species is often greater than the differences between one species and another. texts.full_text: isible groups I'm having a trouble with these problems: For $D$ is an divisible group and $\mathbb{Z}$ is the set of integers prove that $Hom(D,\mathbb{Z})=0$ and $Hom(D,\mathbb{Z_n})=0$ forevery n natural number. Proof: 1. Let's consider a homomorphism f from D to Z, where D is divisible group. Since every element in D can be expressed as d*x, where x belongs to the set of prime numbers and their powers (which we denote P), there must exist an integer k such that f(d) = k0=0 for all elements 0 from Z, it follows that a homomorphism exists only when every element in D is mapped to the zero vector. 2. Now let's consider another case where we need to prove Hom(D,Z_n) is zero. Since n is an arbitrary natural number and each integer can be expressed as s*n + r for some non-negative integers (0<=r<n), a homomorphism from D, which maps d*x to Zn would map the elements of P back to {1,2,...,n-1} because there must exist such m that we have: mr mod n. However, it is impossible since every element in the image set will be mapped either as 0 or a non-zero value and thus can't fulfill both conditions simultaneously

texts.full_text: crater on Mars.Around 75 percent of all Martian meteorites on Earth are referred to as Shergottites. These meteorites are named after a single meteor that crashed at an Indian town named Shergati. A team ofresearchers was able to determine that these Shergottites came from a 34-mile Martian crater called "the Mojave Crater" found in the Oxia Palus quadrangle on Mars. By comparing the Martian meteorites and the material that makes up the Mojave Crater, scientists were able to determine thatmany of the igneous rocks that make up Martian meteorites originated from this specific area of the Red Planet. texts.full_text: 8 shows an exemplary structure of a semiconductor device with three interconnection layers and a fuse. In this semiconductor device, on a surface of a silicon substrate 1 serving as a semiconductor substrate, a field oxide film 10 is selectively formed as an isolating insulating film. Further, a fuse 11 is selectively formed from polycrystalline silicon so as to contact with an upper side of field oxide film 10. Fuse 11 is an element constituting a redundancy circuit for repairing a defective. Through the blow of fuse 11 by a laser, a defective portion can be disconnected and a normal portion can be employed instead, if necessary. texts.full_text: d source region that are both of the same conductivity type as the substrate. When a voltage is applied to the gate that is higher than the threshold voltage, the channel between the source and drain regions becomes conductive, allowing current to flow from the source to the drain. This mode of operation is known as enhancement-mode because it enhances the conductivity of the channel. On the other hand, the depletion-mode MOSFET includes a drain region and a source region that are of opposite conductivity types compared to the substrate. In this case, when no voltage is applied to the gate, the channel between the source and drain regions is already conductive, allowing current to flow. texts.full_text: Greek grammars classified adjectives as a subclass of nouns due to shared inflectional features (gender, number, case). Words like “the,” “this,” and “my,” once termed “adjectives,” are now distinguished as determiners. The shift reflects modern linguistic frameworks reclassifying such modifiers. Originally, “noun adjective” described adjectives, while “noun substantive” denoted nouns proper—a terminology now obsolete. texts.full_text: ion processing, and provides numerous examples illustrating Bayesian approaches to the study of human cognition. We start by providing an overview of Bayesian modeling and Bayesian networks. We then describe three types of information processing operations-inference, parameter learning, and structure learning-in both Bayesian networks and human cognition. this is followed by a discussion of the important roles of prior knowledge and of active learning. We conclude by outlining some challenges for Bayesian models of human cognition that will need to be addressed by future research.
